paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Cinnamon is a sweet, fragrant spice produced from the inner bark of trees of the genus Cinnamomum, which is native to the Indian subcontinent. It was known in biblical times, and is mentioned in several books of the Bible, both as an ingredient that was mixed with oils for anointing people's bodies, and also as a token indicating friendship among lovers and friends. In ancient Rome, mourners attending funerals burnt cinnamon to create a pleasant scent. Most often, however, the spice found its primary use as an additive to food and drink. In the Middle Ages, Europeans who could afford the spice used it to flavour food, particularly meat, and to impress those around them with their ability to purchase an expensive condiment from the 'exotic' East. At a banquet, a host would offer guests a plate with various spices piled upon it as a sign of the wealth at his or her disposal. Cinnamon was also reported to have health benefits, and was thought to cure various ailments, such as indigestion.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Toward the end of the Middle Ages, the European middle classes began to desire the lifestyle of the elite, including their consumption of spices. This led to a growth in demand for cinnamon and other spices. At that time, cinnamon was transported by Arab merchants, who closely guarded the secret of the source of the spice from potential rivals. They took it from India, where it was grown, on camels via an overland route to the Mediterranean. Their journey ended when they reached Alexandria. European traders sailed there to purchase their supply of cinnamon, then brought it back to Venice. The spice then travelled from that great trading city to markets all around Europe. Because the overland trade route allowed for only small quantities of the spice to reach Europe, and because Venice had a virtual monopoly of the trade, the Venetians could set the price of cinnamon exorbitantly high. These prices, coupled with the increasing demand, spurred the search for new routes to Asia by Europeans eager to take part in the spice trade.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Seeking the high profits promised by the cinnamon market, Portuguese traders arrived on the island of Ceylon in the Indian Ocean toward the end of the 15th century. Before Europeans arrived on the island, the state had organized the cultivation of cinnamon. People belonging to the ethnic group called the Salagama would peel the bark off young shoots of the cinnamon plant in the rainy season, when the wet bark was more pliable. During the peeling process, they curled the bark into the 'stick' shape still associated with the spice today. The Salagama then gave the finished product to the king as a form of tribute. When the Portuguese arrived, they needed to increase production significantly, and so enslaved many other members of the Ceylonese native population, forcing them to work in cinnamon harvesting. In 1518, the Portuguese built a fort on Ceylon, which enabled them to protect the island, so helping them to develop a monopoly in the cinnamon trade and generate very high profits. In the late 16th century, for example, they enjoyed a tenfold profit when shipping cinnamon over a journey of eight days from Ceylon to India.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: When the Dutch arrived off the coast of southern Asia at the very beginning of the 17th century, they set their sights on displacing the Portuguese as kings of cinnamon. The Dutch allied themselves with Kandy, an inland kingdom on Ceylon. In return for payments of elephants and cinnamon, they protected the native king from the Portuguese. By 1640, the Dutch broke the 150-year Portuguese monopoly when they overran and occupied their factories. By 1658, they had permanently expelled the Portuguese from the island, thereby gaining control of the lucrative cinnamon trade.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: In order to protect their hold on the market, the Dutch, like the Portuguese before them, treated the native inhabitants harshly. Because of the need to boost production and satisfy Europe's ever-increasing appetite for cinnamon, the Dutch began to alter the harvesting practices of the Ceylonese. Over time, the supply of cinnamon trees on the island became nearly exhausted, due to systematic stripping of the bark. Eventually, the Dutch began cultivating their own cinnamon trees to supplement the diminishing number of wild trees available for use.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Then, in 1796, the English arrived on Ceylon, thereby displacing the Dutch from their control of the cinnamon monopoly. By the middle of the 19th century, production of cinnamon reached 1,000 tons a year, after a lower grade quality of the spice became acceptable to European tastes. By that time, cinnamon was being grown in other parts of the Indian Ocean region and in the West Indies, Brazil, and Guyana. Not only was a monopoly of cinnamon becoming impossible, but the spice trade overall was diminishing in economic potential, and was eventually superseded by the rise of trade in coffee, tea, chocolate, and sugar.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“Biblical times”在原文中直接对应“in biblical times”，而“added to”在原文中对应“mixed with”。因此，答案所在的句子是“...as an ingredient that was mixed with oils for anointing people's bodies...”。由此可见，答案是“oils”。

central_sentences.sentence: It was known in biblical times, and is mentioned in several books of the Bible, both as an ingredient that was mixed with oils for anointing people's bodies, and also as a token indicating friendship among lovers and friends.

central_sentences.sentence: It was known in biblical times, and is mentioned in several books of the Bible, both as an ingredient that was mixed with oils for anointing people's bodies, and also as a token indicating friendship among lovers and friends.

questions.analysis: 题干问的是在古罗马，肉桂因其甜美的香味被用在哪里。通过文章中的句子 "In ancient Rome, mourners attending funerals burnt cinnamon to create a pleasant scent."，可以确认答案为 "funerals"，即葬礼。通过识别并理解关键词和同义替换，学生能够准确地找到正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: In ancient Rome, mourners attending funerals burnt cinnamon to create a pleasant scent.
central_sentences.translation: 在古罗马，参加葬礼的哀悼者会燃烧肉桂以创造宜人的香味。

target_sen.target_sen: In ancient Rome, mourners attending funerals burnt cinnamon to create a pleasant scent.
In ancient Rome, mourners attending funerals burnt cinnamon to create a pleasant scent.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“indication”在原文中对应“sign”，“person's”在原文中对应“his or her”。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以定位到答案句。答案句中明确提到“a sign of the wealth at his or her disposal”，即“wealth”是正确答案，因为它表示一个人拥有的财富。

central_sentences.sentence: At a banquet, a host would offer guests a plate with various spices piled upon it as a sign of the wealth at his or her disposal.
central_sentences.translation: 在宴会上，主人会向客人提供一盘堆满各种香料的盘子，以此作为展示他或她所拥有的财富的标志。

target_sen.target_sen: At a banquet, a host would offer guests a plate with various spices piled upon it as a sign of the wealth at his or her disposal.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是“indigestion”，因为题干中的“treatment for... health problems”在原文中对应的是“cure various ailments”，而“indigestion”是具体列举的疾病之一。通过找到这些同义替换词，可以定位到答案所在的句子。

central_sentences.sentence: Cinnamon was also reported to have health benefits, and was thought to cure various ailments, such as indigestion.
central_sentences.translation: 据报道，肉桂也具有健康益处，被认为可以治疗各种疾病，例如消化不良。

target_sen.target_sen: Cinnamon was also reported to have health benefits, and was thought to cure various ailments, such as indigestion.
Cinnamon was also reported to have health benefits, and was thought to cure various ailments, such as indigestion.

questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词是“grown in”，意思是“生长在”。在文章中，我们找到了与之对应的同义替换词“grown”和“native to”。在句子“They took it from India, where it was grown”中，明确提到肉桂生长在“India”。因此，正确答案是“India”。

central_sentences.sentence: They took it from India, where it was grown, on camels via an overland route to the Mediterranean.
central_sentences.translation: 他们从肉桂生长的地方印度，通过陆路用骆驼将其运送到地中海。

target_sen.target_sen: They took it from India, where it was grown, on camels via an overland route to the Mediterranean.

questions.analysis: 答案是“camels”，因为题干中的“merchants used”对应原文中的“Arab merchants transported”，“to the Mediterranean”在原文中也直接提到。原文明确指出“on camels via an overland route to the Mediterranean”，即“通过陆路用骆驼运到地中海”。因此，骆驼（camels）是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: They took it from India, where it was grown, on camels via an overland route to the Mediterranean.
central_sentences.translation: 他们从印度（肉桂的产地）用骆驼通过陆路将其运送到地中海。

target_sen.target_sen: At that time, cinnamon was transported by Arab merchants, who closely guarded the secret of the source of the spice from potential rivals.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“arrived in the Mediterranean at”可以通过文章中的“reached”与“arrived”同义替换来定位，而“Alexandria”是他们到达地中海的具体地点。因此，正确答案是“Alexandria”。

central_sentences.sentence: Their journey ended when they reached Alexandria.

questions.analysis: 答案是“Venice”，因为文章明确提到欧洲商人将肉桂带到威尼斯，然后从那里卖到欧洲各地。题干中的“traders took it to”对应文章中的“brought it back to”，“sold it to destinations around Europe”对应“markets all around Europe”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案是“Venice”。

central_sentences.sentence: European traders sailed there to purchase their supply of cinnamon, then brought it back to Venice. The spice then travelled from that great trading city to markets all around Europe.

TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,

questions.title: The Portuguese had control over the cinnamon trade in Ceylon throughout the 16th century.

options.text: TRUE
FALSE
NOT GIVEN

questions.analysis: 答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确提到葡萄牙人在1518年建立了堡垒，并在16世纪末享有高利润，这表明他们在整个16世纪期间都控制了肉桂贸易。题干中的 "Portuguese" 对应原文中的 "Portuguese"，"control" 对应 "monopoly"，"cinnamon trade" 对应 "cinnamon trade"，"Ceylon" 对应 "Ceylon"，"16th century" 对应 "16th century" 和 "late 16th century"。这些对应关系表明题干信息在文章中得到了体现。

central_sentences.sentence: In 1518, the Portuguese built a fort on Ceylon, which enabled them to protect the island, so helping them to develop a monopoly in the cinnamon trade and generate very high profits.

central_sentences.sentence: In the late 16th century, for example, they enjoyed a tenfold profit when shipping cinnamon over a journey of eight days from Ceylon to India.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，在16世纪末，他们在将肉桂从锡兰运往印度的8天航程中获得了10倍的利润。

questions.title: The Dutch took over the cinnamon trade from the Portuguese as soon as they arrived in Ceylon.

options.text: TRUE
FALSE
NOT GIVEN

questions.analysis: 题干中的“as soon as they arrived”表示荷兰人一到锡兰就接管了肉桂贸易，而原文中明确指出荷兰人在17世纪初到达亚洲海岸，但直到1640年才打破葡萄牙的垄断，并在1658年才永久驱逐葡萄牙人。这表明荷兰人并不是一到锡兰就接管了肉桂贸易，而是经过了几十年的努力。因此，题干信息与文章内容不符，正确答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.reason: display_answer=F：时间线否定 as soon as。
central_sentences.analysis: 并非一到就接管，而是 1640/1658 才完成。
sentences: By 1640, the Dutch broke the 150-year Portuguese monopoly when they overran and occupied their factories.
By 1658, they had permanently expelled the Portuguese from the island, thereby gaining control of the lucrative cinnamon trade.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: as soon as they arrived -> By 1640/By 1658 (延后); took over -> broke/expelled/gaining control

central_sentences.sentence: By 1640, the Dutch broke the 150-year Portuguese monopoly when they overran and occupied their factories.
By 1658, they had permanently expelled the Portuguese from the island, thereby gaining control of the lucrative cinnamon trade.

questions.title: The trees planted by the Dutch produced larger quantities of cinnamon than the wild trees.

options.text: TRUE
FALSE
NOT GIVEN

questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“荷兰人种植的树木比野生树木产量更高”。在原文中，提到荷兰人开始种植自己的肉桂树以补充野生树木的减少，但并没有提到这些种植的树木是否比野生树木产量更高。因此，题干中的信息在文章中并没有明确提到，所以答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Eventually, the Dutch began cultivating their own cinnamon trees to supplement the diminishing number of wild trees available for use.
central_sentences.translation: 最终，荷兰人开始种植自己的肉桂树，以补充可用的野生树木数量的减少。

syn_ary.synonym: Dutch began cultivating their own cinnamon trees

target_sen.target_sen: Eventually, the Dutch began cultivating their own cinnamon trees to supplement the diminishing number of wild trees available for use.
Eventually, the Dutch began cultivating their own cinnamon trees to supplement the diminishing number of wild trees available for use.

questions.title: The spice trade maintained its economic importance during the 19th century.

options.text: TRUE
FALSE
NOT GIVEN

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "maintained its economic importance" 表示香料贸易在19世纪保持了其经济重要性，而原文中明确提到 "the spice trade overall was diminishing in economic potential"（香料贸易的整体经济潜力在下降），这与题干的陈述相反。因此，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Not only was a monopoly of cinnamon becoming impossible, but the spice trade overall was diminishing in economic potential, and was eventually superseded by the rise of trade in coffee, tea, chocolate, and sugar.
central_sentences.translation: 不仅肉桂的垄断变得不可能，而且整个香料贸易的经济潜力正在减弱，最终被咖啡、茶、巧克力和糖的贸易兴起所取代。

target_sen.target_sen: Not only was a monopoly of cinnamon becoming impossible, but the spice trade overall was diminishing in economic potential, and was eventually superseded by the rise of trade in coffee, tea, chocolate, and sugar.

content.title: Oxytocin
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲了催产素的作用和影响，它可以加强动物之间的联系，加强母亲和婴儿之间的依恋，但最新研究表明，催产素的效果因人而异，可能对社交互动产生负面影响。', 'B': '这段话主要讲了催产素在人类行为中的作用，通过一项开创性的实验发现，催产素可以增加人们的慈善行为、提高读取他人情绪和在争论中进行建设性沟通的能力，从而普遍增强了我们社交本性的积极方面。', 'C': '这段话主要讲了关于催产素的研究发现，催产素在不同情境下会产生截然相反的效果，对个体的性格特点也有不同的影响。', 'D': '这段话主要讲了催产素的效果因与我们互动的对象而异。研究发现，接受催产素剂量的人在与陌生人打交道时变得不太合作；同时，接受催产素的志愿者表现出偏袒：荷兰男性更倾向于将积极的词语与荷兰名字联系起来，而不是与外国名字联系起来。因此，催产素似乎加强了偏见，而不是促进普遍的善意。', 'E': '这段话主要讲了关于催产素的研究发现，指出催产素的影响并不是普遍的，而是在特定个体或特定情况下产生作用，因此需要更加细致入微地理解催产素的效应，并找出其核心功能，而不是简单地列举其无限的效应。催产素可能有助于减少焦虑和恐惧，也可能仅仅是激励人们寻求社交联系，它作为一种化学聚光灯，使人们更加敏锐地察觉社交环境中的线索，从而解释了为什么它使我们更有可能与他人进行眼神交流，并提高了我们识别情绪的能力，但对于过于敏感或倾向于以最坏的方式解读社交线索的人来说，催产素可能会使情况变得更糟。', 'F': '这段话主要讲了催产素的复杂性和多功能性，它在各种动物中存在，并且具有古老的进化根源，对大脑的原始部分产生影响，可能会在不同的个体差异和环境背景下表现出不同的方式。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Oxytocin is a chemical, a hormone produced in the pituitary gland in the brain. It was through various studies focusing on animals that scientists first became aware of the influence of oxytocin. They discovered that it helps reinforce the bonds between prairie voles, which mate for life, and triggers the motherly behaviour that sheep show towards their newborn lambs. It is also released by women in childbirth, strengthening the attachment between mother and baby. Few chemicals have as positive a reputation as oxytocin, which is sometimes referred to as the 'love hormone'. One sniff of it can, it is claimed, make a person more trusting, empathetic, generous and cooperative. It is time, however, to revise this wholly optimistic view. A new wave of studies has shown that its effects vary greatly depending on the person and the circumstances, and it can impact on our social interactions for worse as well as for better.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Oxytocin's role in human behaviour first emerged in 2005. In a groundbreaking experiment, Markus Heinrichs and his colleagues at the University of Freiburg, Germany, asked volunteers to do an activity in which they could invest money with an anonymous person who was not guaranteed to be honest. The team found that participants who had sniffed oxytocin via a nasal spray beforehand invested more money than those who received a placebo instead. The study was the start of research into the effects of oxytocin on human interactions. 'For eight years, it was quite a lonesome field,' Heinrichs recalls. 'Now, everyone is interested.' These follow-up studies have shown that after a sniff of the hormone, people become more charitable, better at reading emotions on others' faces and at communicating constructively in arguments. Together, the results fuelled the view that oxytocin universally enhanced the positive aspects of our social nature.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Then, after a few years, contrasting findings began to emerge. Simone Shamay-Tsoory at the University of Haifa, Israel, found that when volunteers played a competitive game, those who inhaled the hormone showed more pleasure when they beat other players, and felt more envy when others won. What's more, administering oxytocin also has sharply contrasting outcomes depending on a person's disposition. Jennifer Bartz from Mount Sinai School of Medicine, New York, found that it improves people's ability to read emotions, but only if they are not very socially adept to begin with. Her research also shows that oxytocin in fact reduces cooperation in subjects who are particularly anxious or sensitive to rejection.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Another discovery is that oxytocin's effects vary depending on who we are interacting with. Studies conducted by Carolyn DeClerck of the University of Antwerp, Belgium, revealed that people who had received a dose of oxytocin actually became less cooperative when dealing with complete strangers. Meanwhile, Carsten De Dreu at the University of Amsterdam in the Netherlands discovered that volunteers given oxytocin showed favouritism: Dutch men became quicker to associate positive words with Dutch names than with foreign ones, for example. According to De Dreu, oxytocin drives people to care for those in their social circles and defend them from outside dangers. So, it appears that oxytocin strengthens biases, rather than promoting general goodwill, as was previously thought.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: There were signs of these subtleties from the start. Bartz has recently shown that in almost half of the existing research results, oxytocin influenced only certain individuals or in certain circumstances. Where once researchers took no notice of such findings, now a more nuanced understanding of oxytocin's effects is propelling investigations down new lines. To Bartz, the key to understanding what the hormone does lies in pinpointing its core function rather than in cataloguing its seemingly endless effects. There are several hypotheses which are not mutually exclusive. Oxytocin could help to reduce anxiety and fear. Or it could simply motivate people to seek out social connections. She believes that oxytocin acts as a chemical spotlight that shines on social clues - a shift in posture, a flicker of the eyes, a dip in the voice - making people more attuned to their social environment. This would explain why it makes us more likely to look others in the eye and improves our ability to identify emotions. But it could also make things worse for people who are overly sensitive or prone to interpreting social cues in the worst light.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Perhaps we should not be surprised that the oxytocin story has become more perplexing. The hormone is found in everything from octopuses to sheep, and its evolutionary roots stretch back half a billion years. 'It's a very simple and ancient molecule that has been co-opted for many different functions,' says Sue Carter at the University of Illinois, Chicago, USA. 'It affects primitive parts of the brain like the amygdala, so it's going to have many effects on just about everything.' Bartz agrees. 'Oxytocin probably does some very basic things, but once you add our higher-order thinking and social situations, these basic processes could manifest in different ways depending on individual differences and context.'

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Most managers can identify the major trends of the day. But in the course of conducting research in a number of industries and working directly with companies, we have discovered that managers often fail to recognize the less obvious but profound ways these trends are influencing consumers' aspirations, attitudes, and behaviors. This is especially true of trends that managers view as peripheral to their core markets.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Many ignore trends in their innovation strategies or adopt a wait-and-see approach and let competitors take the lead. At a minimum, such responses mean missed profit opportunities. At the extreme, they can jeopardize a company by ceding to rivals the opportunity to transform the industry. The purpose of this article is twofold: to spur managers to think more expansively about how trends could engender new value propositions in their core markets, and to provide some high-level advice on how to make market research and product development personnel more adept at analyzing and exploiting trends.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: One strategy, known as 'infuse and augment', is to design a product or service that retains most of the attributes and functions of existing products in the category but adds others that address the needs and desires unleashed by a major trend. A case in point is the Poppy range of handbags, which the firm Coach created in response to the economic downturn of 2008. The Coach brand had been a symbol of opulence and luxury for nearly 70 years, and the most obvious reaction to the downturn would have been to lower prices. However, that would have risked cheapening the brand's image. Instead, they initiated a consumer-research project which revealed that customers were eager to lift themselves and the country out of tough times. Using these insights, Coach launched the lower-priced Poppy handbags, which were in vibrant colors, and looked more youthful and playful than conventional Coach products. Creating the sub-brand allowed Coach to avert an across-the-board price cut. In contrast to the many companies that responded to the recession by cutting prices, Coach saw the new consumer mindset as an opportunity for innovation and renewal.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: A further example of this strategy was supermarket Tesco's response to consumers' growing concerns about the environment. With that in mind, Tesco, one of the world's top five retailers, introduced its Greener Living program, which demonstrates the company's commitment to protecting the environment by involving consumers in ways that produce tangible results. For example, Tesco customers can accumulate points for such activities as reusing bags, recycling cans and printer cartridges, and buying home-insulation materials. Like points earned on regular purchases, these green points can be redeemed for cash. Tesco has not abandoned its traditional retail offerings but augmented its business with these innovations, thereby infusing its value proposition with a green streak.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: A more radical strategy is 'combine and transcend'. This entails combining aspects of the product's existing value proposition with attributes addressing changes arising from a trend, to create a novel experience—one that may land the company in an entirely new market space. At first glance, spending resources to incorporate elements of a seemingly irrelevant trend into one's core offerings sounds like it's hardly worthwhile. But consider Nike's move to integrate the digital revolution into its reputation for high-performance athletic footwear. In 2006, they teamed up with technology company Apple to launch Nike+, a digital sports kit comprising a sensor that attaches to the running shoe and a wireless receiver that connects to the user's iPod. By combining Nike's original value proposition for amateur athletes with one for digital consumers, the Nike+ sports kit and web interface moved the company from a focus on athletic apparel to a new plane of engagement with its customers.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: A third approach, known as 'counteract and reaffirm', involves developing products or services that stress the values traditionally associated with the category in ways that allow consumers to oppose—or at least temporarily escape from—the aspects of trends they view as undesirable. A product that accomplished this is the ME2, a video game created by Canada's iToys. By reaffirming the toy category's association with physical play, the ME2 counteracted some of the widely perceived negative impacts of digital gaming devices. Like other handheld games, the device featured a host of exciting interactive games, a full-color LCD screen, and advanced 3D graphics. What set it apart was that it incorporated the traditional physical component of children's play: it contained a pedometer, which tracked and awarded points for physical activity (walking, running, biking, skateboarding, climbing stairs). The child could use the points to enhance various virtual skills needed for the video game. The ME2, introduced in mid-2008, catered to kids' huge desire to play video games while countering the negatives, such as associations with lack of exercise and obesity.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Once you have gained perspective on how trend-related changes in consumer opinions and behaviors impact on your category, you can determine which of our three innovation strategies to pursue. When your category's basic value proposition continues to be meaningful for consumers influenced by the trend, the infuse-and-augment strategy will allow you to reinvigorate the category. If analysis reveals an increasing disparity between your category and consumers' new focus, your innovations need to transcend the category to integrate the two worlds. Finally, if aspects of the category clash with undesired outcomes of a trend, such as associations with unhealthy lifestyles, there is an opportunity to counteract those changes by reaffirming the core values of your category.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Trends—technological, economic, environmental, social, or political—that affect how people perceive the world around them and shape what they expect from products and services present firms with unique opportunities for growth.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-31

Choose the correct answer, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: In the first paragraph, the writer says that most managers

options.text: fail to spot the key consumer trends of the moment.
make the mistake of focusing only on the principal consumer trends.
misinterpret market research data relating to current consumer trends.
are unaware of the significant impact that trends have on consumers' lives.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是D，因为文章明确指出管理者未能意识到这些趋势对消费者生活的深远影响。题干中的“unaware of the significant impact that trends have on consumers' lives”与文章中的“fail to recognize the less obvious but profound ways these trends are influencing consumers' aspirations, attitudes, and behaviors”是同义表达。

central_sentences.sentence: But in the course of conducting research in a number of industries and working directly with companies, we have discovered that managers often fail to recognize the less obvious but profound ways these trends are influencing consumers' aspirations, attitudes, and behaviors.
central_sentences.translation: 但是，在对多个行业进行研究并直接与公司合作的过程中，我们发现管理者经常无法识别这些趋势以不太明显但深远的方式影响消费者愿望、态度和行为的情况。

questions.title: According to the third paragraph, Coach was anxious to

options.text: follow what some of its competitors were doing.
maintain its prices throughout its range.
safeguard its reputation as a manufacturer of luxury goods.
modify the entire look of its brand to suit the economic climate.

questions.analysis: 根据文章内容，Coach在经济衰退期间选择不降价，以避免损害其奢侈品形象。这表明他们希望保护其作为奢侈品制造商的声誉。因此，正确答案是C选项：safeguard its reputation as a manufacturer of luxury goods。

central_sentences.sentence: The Coach brand had been a symbol of opulence and luxury for nearly 70 years, and the most obvious reaction to the downturn would have been to lower prices. However, that would have risked cheapening the brand's image.
central_sentences.translation: Coach品牌近70年来一直是奢华和豪华的象征，面对经济衰退最明显的反应本应是降价。然而，这样做可能会降低品牌形象。

target_sen.target_sen: However, that would have risked cheapening the brand's image.

questions.title: What point is made about Tesco's Greener Living programme?

options.text: It did not require Tesco to modify its core business activities.
It succeeded in attracting a more eco-conscious clientele.
Its main aim was to raise consumers' awareness of environmental issues.
It was not the first time that Tesco had implemented such an initiative.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是A，因为文章明确指出Tesco并没有放弃其传统的零售业务，而是通过Greener Living计划增强了其业务。这与选项A的意思完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Tesco has not abandoned its traditional retail offerings but augmented its business with these innovations, thereby infusing its value proposition with a green streak.
central_sentences.translation: 乐购并没有放弃其传统的零售业务，而是通过这些创新来增强其业务，从而为其价值主张注入了环保元素。

target_sen.target_sen: With that in mind, Tesco, one of the world's top five retailers, introduced its Greener Living program, which demonstrates the company's commitment to protecting the environment by involving consumers in ways that produce tangible results.

questions.title: What does the writer suggest about Nike's strategy?

options.text: It was an extremely risky strategy at the time.
It was a strategy that only a major company could afford to follow.
It was the type of strategy that would not have been possible in the past.
It was the kind of strategy which might appear to have few obvious benefits.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到Nike的策略“乍一看，花费资源将看似无关的趋势元素融入核心产品似乎不值得”，这与题干中的“might appear to have few obvious benefits”意思相近，表明这种策略表面上看起来似乎没什么明显的好处。因此，正确答案是D。

central_sentences.sentence: At first glance, spending resources to incorporate elements of a seemingly irrelevant trend into one's core offerings sounds like it's hardly worthwhile.
central_sentences.translation: 乍看之下，将资源用于将看似无关的趋势元素融入公司的核心产品中，似乎并不值得。

target_sen.target_sen: A more radical strategy is 'combine and transcend's.

questions.title: What was original about the ME2?

options.text: It contained technology that had been developed for the sports industry.
It appealed to young people who were keen to improve their physical fitness.
It took advantage of a current trend for video games with colourful 3D graphics.
It was a handheld game that addressed people's concerns about unhealthy lifestyles.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 D，因为文章明确指出 ME2 是一款手持游戏，通过包括体力活动元素来解决与不健康生活方式相关的问题。这与选项 D 的描述完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: The ME2, introduced in mid- 2008, catered to kids' huge desire to play video games while countering the negatives, such as associations with lack of exercise and obesity.
central_sentences.translation: ME2于2008年年中推出，满足了孩子们对玩视频游戏的巨大渴望，同时也解决了相关的负面影响，如缺乏运动和肥胖等问题。

groups.title: List of companies
groups.desc:
Questions 32-37

Look at the following statements (Questions 32-37) and the list of companies below
Match each statement with the correct company.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

questions.title: It turned the notion that its products could have harmful effects to its own advantage.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 D.iToys。题干中的 "turned the notion that its products could have harmful effects to its own advantage" 意思是将产品可能有害的概念转化为优势。文章中提到 iToys 的 ME2 视频游戏通过重申玩具与体力活动的关联，抵消了数字游戏设备的负面影响（如缺乏运动和肥胖），这正是将负面影响转化为优势的例子。

central_sentences.sentence: By reaffirming the toy category's association with physical play, the ME2 counteracted some of the widely perceived negative impacts of digital gaming devices.
central_sentences.translation: 通过重申玩具类别与体育活动的联系，ME2抵消了人们普遍认为的数字游戏设备的一些负面影响。

target_sen.target_sen: By reaffirming the toy category's association with physical play, the ME2 counteracted some of the widely perceived negative impacts of digital gaming devices.
By reaffirming the toy category's association with physical play, the ME2 counteracted some of the widely perceived negative impacts of digital gaming devices.

questions.title: It extended its offering by collaborating with another manufacturer.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“collaborating with another manufacturer”在原文中对应的表达是“teamed up with technology company Apple”。耐克与苹果合作推出了Nike+，这正是题干所描述的“与另一制造商合作扩展其产品”的意思。因此，正确答案是C: Nike。

central_sentences.sentence: In 2006, they teamed up with technology company Apple to launch Nike+, a digital sports kit comprising a sensor that attaches to the running shoe and a wireless receiver that connects to the user's iPod. By
central_sentences.translation: 2006年，他们与科技公司苹果合作推出了Nike+，这是一套数字运动套件，包括一个附在跑鞋上的传感器和一个连接到用户iPod的无线接收器。

target_sen.target_sen: In 2006, they teamed up with technology company Apple to launch Nike+, a digital sports kit comprising a sensor that attaches to the running shoe and a wireless receiver that connects to the user's iPod.

syn_ary.synonym: integrate the digital revolution into its reputation for high-performance athletic footwear

target_sen.target_sen: But consider Nike's move to integrate the digital revolution into its reputation for high-performance athletic footwear.

questions.title: It implemented an incentive scheme to demonstrate its corporate social responsibility.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "incentive scheme" 和 "corporate social responsibility" 对应原文中的 "accumulate points" 和 "commitment to protecting the environment"。通过这些同义表达，可以确认题干描述的内容与文章中关于Tesco的描述相符，因此正确答案是B.Tesco。

central_sentences.sentence: With that in mind, Tesco, one of the world's top five retailers, introduced its Greener Living program, which demonstrates the company's commitment to protecting the environment by involving consumers in ways that produce tangible results. For example, Tesco customers can accumulate points for such activities as reusing bags, recycling cans and printer cartridges, and buying home-insulation materials. Like points earned on regular purchases, these green points can be redeemed for cash.
central_sentences.translation: 考虑到这一点，全球五大零售商之一的乐购公司推出了"绿色生活"计划，该计划通过让消费者以产生切实结果的方式参与进来，展示了公司保护环境的承诺。例如，乐购的顾客可以通过重复使用购物袋、回收罐子和打印机墨盒，以及购买家居隔热材料等活动来积累积分。与常规购物获得的积分一样，这些绿色积分可以兑换现金。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, Tesco customers can accumulate points for such activities as reusing bags, recycling cans and printer cartridges, and buying home-insulation materials.

With that in mind, Tesco, one of the world's top five retailers, introduced its Greener Living program, which demonstrates the company's commitment to protecting the environment by involving consumers in ways that produce tangible results.

questions.title: It discovered that customers had a positive attitude towards dealing with difficult circumstances.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "discovered" 和 "positive attitude towards dealing with difficult circumstances" 在原文中分别对应 "revealed" 和 "eager to lift themselves and the country out of tough times"。这些同义表达明确指出了 Coach 在经济衰退期间通过消费者研究发现顾客的积极态度，因此正确答案是 A: Coach。

central_sentences.sentence: Instead, they initiated a consumer-research project which revealed that customers were eager to lift themselves and the country out of tough times.
central_sentences.translation: 相反，他们启动了一项消费者研究项目，该项目揭示顾客渴望让自己和国家摆脱困境。

target_sen.target_sen: Instead, they initiated a consumer-research project which revealed that customers were eager to lift themselves and the country out of tough times.

syn_ary.synonym: eager to lift themselves and the country out of tough times

target_sen.target_sen: Instead, they initiated a consumer-research project which revealed that customers were eager to lift themselves and the country out of tough times.

locating_words.locating_word: positive attitude towards dealing with difficult circumstances

questions.title: It responded to a growing lifestyle trend in an unrelated product sector.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“growing lifestyle trend”在原文中对应的是“digital revolution”，而“unrelated product sector”对应的是“new plane of engagement”或“entirely new market space”。耐克通过将数字革命整合到其运动鞋产品中，进入了一个全新的市场空间，这正是题干所描述的情况。因此，正确答案是C. Nike。

central_sentences.sentence: By combining Nike's original value proposition for amateur athletes with one for digital consumers, the Nike+ sports kit and web interface moved the company from a focus on athletic apparel to a new plane of engagement with its customers.
central_sentences.translation: 通过将耐克面向业余运动员的原有价值主张与面向数字消费者的价值主张相结合，Nike+运动套件和网络界面使公司从专注于运动服装转向了与客户互动的新层面。

target_sen.target_sen: But consider Nike's move to integrate the digital revolution into its reputation for high-performance athletic footwear.

By combining Nike's original value proposition for amateur athletes with one for digital consumers, the Nike+ sports kit and web interface moved the company from a focus on athletic apparel to a new plane of engagement with its customers.

questions.title: It successfully avoided having to charge its customers less for its core products.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "avoided" 和 "charge less" 在原文中对应的同义词分别是 "avert" 和 "price cut"。通过这些关键词的匹配，可以确定答案句是 "Creating the sub-brand allowed Coach to avert an across-the-board price cut." 这句话明确指出了 Coach 通过创建子品牌避免了降价，因此正确答案是 A: Coach。

central_sentences.sentence: Creating the sub-brand allowed Coach to avert an across-the-board price cut.
central_sentences.translation: 创建子品牌使得Coach能够避免全面降价。

target_sen.target_sen: Creating the sub-brand allowed Coach to avert an across-the-board price cut.

groups.desc:
Questions 38-40

Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: employ a combination of strategies to maintain your consumer base.
identify the most appropriate innovation strategy to use.
emphasise your brand's traditional values with the counteract-and- affirm strategy.
use the combine-and-transcend strategy to integrate the two worlds.

questions.title: If there are any trend-related changes impacting on your category, you should
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为文章明确指出在了解趋势变化对你的类别的影响后，你需要决定采用哪种创新策略。这与选项B“identify the most appropriate innovation strategy to use”直接对应。文章中的“determine which of our three innovation strategies to pursue”与选项B中的“identify the most appropriate innovation strategy”是同义表达。

central_sentences.sentence: Once you have gained perspective on how trend-related changes in consumer opinions and behaviors impact on your category, you can determine which of our three innovation strategies to pursue.
central_sentences.translation: 一旦你了解了消费者观点和行为的趋势相关变化如何影响你的类别，你就可以决定采用我们三种创新策略中的哪一种。

target_sen.target_sen: Once you have gained perspective on how trend-related changes in consumer opinions and behaviors impact on your category, you can determine which of our three innovation strategies to pursue.
Once you have gained perspective on how trend-related changes in consumer opinions and behaviors impact on your category, you can determine which of our three innovation strategies to pursue.

questions.title: If a current trend highlights a negative aspect of your category, you should
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "negative aspect" 对应文章中的 "undesired outcomes"，而 "you should" 对应文章中的 "there is an opportunity to"。文章明确指出，当类别的某些方面与趋势的不良结果发生冲突时，可以通过 "counteract and reaffirm" 策略来应对。因此，正确答案是 C。

central_sentences.sentence: Finally, if aspects of the category clash with undesired outcomes of a trend, such as associations with unhealthy lifestyles, there is an opportunity to counteract those changes by reaffirming the core values of your category.
central_sentences.translation: 最后，如果某个类别的某些方面与趋势的不良结果相冲突，例如与不健康生活方式的联系，那么就有机会通过重申你的类别的核心价值来抵消这些变化。

target_sen.target_sen: Finally, if aspects of the category clash with undesired outcomes of a trend, such as associations with unhealthy lifestyles, there is an opportunity to counteract those changes by reaffirming the core values of your category.

questions.title: If the consumers'new focus has an increasing lack of connection with your offering, you should
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "increasing lack of connection" 和 "your offering" 在原文中分别对应 "increasing disparity" 和 "your category"。原文明确指出，当消费者的新关注点与现有产品之间的差距越来越大时，需要采用 "combine and transcend" 策略来整合两者。因此，正确答案是 D。

central_sentences.sentence: If analysis reveals an increasing disparity between your category and consumers, new focus, your innovations need to transcend the category to integrate the two worlds. Finally
central_sentences.translation: 如果分析显示你的产品类别与消费者的新关注点之间存在越来越大的差距，你的创新就需要超越原有类别，以整合这两个世界。

target_sen.target_sen: If analysis reveals an increasing disparity between your category and consumers, new focus, your innovations need to transcend the category to integrate the two worlds.
Once you have gained perspective on how trend-related changes in consumer opinions and behaviors impact on your category, you can determine which of our three innovation strategies to pursue.
When your category's basic value proposition continues to be meaningful for consumers influenced by the trend, the infuse-and-augment strategy will allow you to reinvigorate the category.
If analysis reveals an increasing disparity between your category and consumers, new focus, your innovations need to transcend the category to integrate the two worlds.

Finally, if aspects of the category clash with undesired outcomes of a trend, such as associations with unhealthy lifestyles, there is an opportunity to counteract those changes by reaffirming the core values of your category.
